spec_match.rationale: Encodes size/intent/state variants entirely in CSS via if(style(--var: value)) — the canonical `single class + custom property` pattern the feature index calls out.

interactive.rationale: 15 buttons (per probe) drive variant selection across two components and re-style live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises one-class no-JS variants; the page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: an unsupported-arm fallback story, hover/active state composition, dark-mode interaction.

references.rationale: Page has NO `references` or `see also` section. CLAUDE.md §3 requires the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link.

design_system.rationale: The JS fallback path bakes raw hex literals (`#fce8e8`, `#e8f8f0`, `#fdf3dc`, `#7a0000`, `#004d28`) instead of design system tokens or page-local custom properties. CLAUDE.md §5 forbids raw hex in concept pages.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex in JS fallbacks
openQuestions.detail: The variant-fallback maps in JS use raw hex (`#fce8e8`, `#7a0000` etc). Define page-local custom properties (`--danger-bg`, `--danger-fg`) up top and reference them, or use the design system tokens. CLAUDE.md §5.

openQuestions.title: Show dark mode + variants composition
openQuestions.detail: if(style()) is most useful when combined with if(media()). Add a dark-mode toggle that re-themes the variants through nested if().

summary: Functional variant system; the missing references block and raw-hex fallback palette are both repo-rule regressions.
